description_long: This is a long-handled pitchfork featuring three sharply pointed metal tines, each evenly spaced and slightly curved outward for effective lifting or turning of materials. The tines are attached to a robust central shaft, which appears to be made of metal and extends down to a pointed, diamond-shaped end cap. The shaft is slender and straight, with a subtle metallic sheen and a muted, dark olive coloration, suggesting it is designed for durability and strength. The transition between the tines and the handle is reinforced with a crossbar, providing additional structural support. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible grip covering or ergonomic enhancements at the handle end. This pitchfork would be suitable for use in urban gardens, parks, or maintenance areas where sturdy, reliable tools are required.
description: A long, straight metal pitchfork with three pointed tines and a diamond-shaped end cap, finished in dark olive tones.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing three evenly spaced, pointed tines and the full length of the straight handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the tines and shaft, with minimal width visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the three tines and straight handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and alignment of the tines and shaft.
